title: In Defense of Selective Colleges
subtitle: 上名校到底有没有用？为精英大学正名

paragraphs.en: Selective colleges and universities in the U.S. are under fire for being too elite and too expensive, and for not training graduates for the world of work. Such charges ignore the fact that these institutions continue to prepare students for success in their work, for thoughtful engagement in civic life, for lifelong learning, and for understanding the world and those with whom they live. These colleges and universities must be doing something right. Applications are at record highs, and their financial aid programs make them more accessible than ever.
paragraphs.zh: 美国的精英学院和大学因过于精英化、学费过高以及未能为职场培养毕业生而受到抨击。这些指责忽视了这样一个事实：这些机构仍在帮助学生在工作中取得成功、深思熟虑地参与公民生活、终身学习以及理解世界和与己共处的人们。这些院校一定做对了一些事。申请人数创下历史新高，而它们的经济资助计划使入学比以往任何时候都更加容易。

words.meaning: phr. 受到攻击，遭受批评

paragraphs.en: This model of education has long played a central role in creating opportunity, driving economic growth, and spurring innovation. Yet, there is growing skepticism about the value of this model. The recent tax reform bill is a wake-up call that our strongest colleges and universities are under assault by some in government. The initial proposals would have made education unaffordable for many by taxing tuition waivers for graduate students and ending deductions for student loan interest.
paragraphs.zh: 这种教育模式长期以来在创造机会、推动经济增长和激发创新方面发挥着核心作用。然而，人们对这一模式的价值越来越怀疑。最近的税收改革法案敲响了警钟：我们最强大的学院和大学正受到政府中某些人的攻击。最初的提案将对研究生学费减免征税，并取消学生贷款利息扣除，使许多人无力承担教育费用。

paragraphs.en: Thankfully, these provisions were ultimately stripped from the bill, but lawmakers let stand a new tax on the investment income of some colleges and universities. While these attacks are motivated by misguided ideas, we need to do a better job of explaining why these claims are false and why what we do is valuable. We cannot take for granted that any of this is obvious.
paragraphs.zh: 值得庆幸的是，这些条款最终被从法案中删除，但立法者保留了针对部分院校投资收入的一项新税。虽然这些攻击源于被误导的想法，但我们需要更好地解释为何这些说法是错误的，以及我们所做之事为何有价值。我们不能理所当然地认为这一切都是显而易见的。

paragraphs.en: It is often said that elite colleges and universities do not train students, particularly those who study the liberal arts, for the workforce. But this can be refuted by scholarly research. The data are clear: a liberal arts education is great career preparation, both for excellent lifetime earnings and for satisfaction with the work. This education develops the skills of critical thinking, rigorous analysis of data and facts, communication with the written and spoken word, understanding of cultural differences and issues, and the ability to keep learning. In fact, liberal arts graduates do extremely well in every imaginable field.
paragraphs.zh: 人们常说精英学院和大学不为职场培养学生，尤其是文科生。但这可以被学术研究所反驳。数据很清楚：文科教育是极好的职业准备，无论对于丰厚的终身收入还是工作满意度都是如此。这种教育培养了批判性思维、对数据和事实的严格分析、书面和口头交流、对文化差异和问题的理解以及持续学习的能力。事实上，文科毕业生在每个能想到的领域都表现得极其出色。

paragraphs.en: Access to an education at selective colleges and universities is now more available than ever to low- and middle-income families. We have built endowments from donations by alumni and parents who understand and appreciate our mission to provide access and opportunity, and a significant portion of the returns from these endowments is used to fund financial aid. Ironically, the new tax on endowments drains financial aid funds from the very schools most able to offer opportunity to those who have earned a spot but cannot otherwise afford this education. Beyond the virtue of access to those who have earned a place at these schools, the diversity of economic backgrounds enhances the education and experience of all of our students.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，低收入和中等收入家庭比以往任何时候都更容易进入精英院校接受教育。我们通过校友和家长的捐赠建立了基金，他们理解并赞赏我们提供机会和途径的使命，而这些基金收益的很大一部分用于资助经济援助。具有讽刺意味的是，新的基金税恰恰从那些最有能力为凭实力获得名额却无力负担学费的学生提供机会的学校抽走了助学金。除了让有资格者得以入学这一优点外，经济背景的多样性也丰富了所有学生的教育和体验。
